<!-- Auto-generated by the stack-research-2026 workflow (verified, adversarially checked).
     Reference for Phases 2-7. The Auth/App Check/Firestore (Phase 6) topic did NOT finish
     its verification pass (pipeline[3] failed) and must be re-researched before Phase 6. -->

# Portfolio Website 2026 — Implementation Reference

**Stack:** Next.js 16 (App Router, `src/`, `proxy.ts`) · next-intl v4 · next-mdx-remote-client v2 (runtime MDX) · Firebase App Hosting + Firestore + Anonymous Auth + App Check · Gemini API (`@google/genai`) · Tailwind v4 · Noto fonts

This document synthesizes verified research. Claims marked **confirmed** by official docs are presented as fact; **refuted**/**uncertain** items use the correction and are flagged with ⚠ **Residual uncertainty**.

---

## ⚠ Must-fix before implementing (aggregated)

These are the load-bearing items that will break or surprise you on this exact stack. Resolve every one before writing production code.

### A. Firebase App Hosting

1. **`runConfig.cpu` default is `0`, not `1`** (refuted claim). With `cpu < 1` you **must** set `concurrency: 1`, and CPU is only allocated during request processing. If you want a guaranteed full CPU, set `cpu: 1` explicitly in `apphosting.yaml`. ([configure docs](https://firebase.google.com/docs/app-hosting/configure))
2. **Re-verify Next.js 16 support** (refuted claim). Official docs say "Next.js 13.5.x and higher" with **no upper bound** and **no** mention of a "15.2.x matrix" or of 16 being excluded. The "Next.js 16 unsupported" claim is **not substantiated**. The project is already scaffolded on Next.js 16 — confirm against the App Hosting framework support schedule before relying on it. ([about App Hosting](https://firebase.google.com/docs/app-hosting/about-app-hosting))
3. **Do not rely on "Developer Connect OAuth" naming** (uncertain). The get-started page does not name Developer Connect; it only says "Connect to GitHub" as a browser/console step. Confirm the exact mechanism + any prerequisite APIs against the dedicated GitHub-integration doc.
4. **"Skip the browser entirely" is overstated** (uncertain). `firebase init apphosting` only **generates `apphosting.yaml`** — it does not create the backend or connect GitHub. Backend creation + GitHub connection is a console flow. `firebase deploy` is **not** the documented primary deploy path for App Hosting (rollouts are GitHub-push driven).
5. **"Secrets cannot back `NEXT_PUBLIC_`" is an unverified inference.** Docs don't state this rule. Documented safe pattern: declare `NEXT_PUBLIC_` vars as plain `value` entries with `availability` including `BUILD`; reserve `secret` for server-side runtime secrets.
6. **Externally-created secrets need explicit access grant.** Secrets made directly in Cloud Secret Manager require `firebase apphosting:secrets:grantaccess`; access is **not** automatic.

### B. next-intl v4

7. **`getRequestConfig` MUST return `locale`** (v4 breaking change). It was optional in v3; omitting it now throws.
8. **Sitemap `getPathname` is async in the official example.** The doc `await`s `getPathname` inside an `async function sitemap()`. Either await it (and make `sitemap` async) or confirm your installed version's `getPathname` is synchronous before using a non-async form.
9. **Verify next-intl v4 peer-dependency range against Next.js 16** (open). The v4 blog/docs do not state an explicit minimum Next.js version. Run `npm ls next next-intl` / check `peerDependencies` before building.
10. **`createNextIntlPlugin` path is optional.** With the file at `src/i18n/request.ts` it is auto-detected, so `createNextIntlPlugin()` with no argument works. Don't treat the explicit path as mandatory.
11. **Decide URL scheme before adding `pathnames`.** For locale prefixes with English slugs (the stated goal), **omit `pathnames` entirely**. Only add it to localize static segments (e.g. `/es/noticias`).

### C. next-mdx-remote-client v2

12. **Pin Node `>= 20.9.0`**, not 18.18. The published 2.1.11 manifest sets `engines.node ">=20.9.0"`. A stale README line mentioning 18.18+ is misleading. Your runtime/CI (and `apphosting.yaml` Node version) must target 20.9.0+.
13. **Place `disableImports`/`disableExports` at the TOP LEVEL of options** (`EvaluateOptions`), **not** inside `mdxOptions`. Under `mdxOptions` they silently do nothing — defeating the RCE-mitigation/translation-validation goal for untrusted Firestore content.
14. **Always pass the `components` map explicitly** on every RSC call. `MDXProvider` is **effectless** in the App Router (RSCs can't read context). Missing custom tags throw unless you also add `recma-mdx-escape-missing-components`.
15. **Add a React `ErrorBoundary` around rendered `{content}`** in addition to `onError`/the `error` field. The internal eval only throws for **syntax** errors; render-time errors are not captured.
16. **Runtime MDX needs `'unsafe-eval'` in CSP `script-src`** (engineering guidance, not a documented library guarantee). A strict CSP without it will break runtime MDX evaluation. Test under your actual Next 16 build.
17. **Turbopack behavior under Next 16 is undocumented** for this library. Run a real Next 16 + Turbopack build rendering RSC MDX (especially dynamic component imports inside MDX) before committing the architecture.

### D. Gemini API

18. **Decide the backend path explicitly:** Gemini Developer API (AI Studio key, `apiKey`-based) **vs** Vertex AI on Google Cloud (ADC + project/location, **no plain apiKey**). The same `@google/genai` SDK is configured differently for each. The "Google Cloud API key" phrasing is ambiguous — for the simple `apiKey`-from-env approach, use an **AI Studio Gemini API key**.
19. **Pass `apiKey` explicitly.** The SDK auto-reads `GEMINI_API_KEY` **or** `GOOGLE_API_KEY`, and `GOOGLE_API_KEY` **wins if both are set**. Explicit passing avoids silent auth surprises.
20. **Server-side only.** Never ship the key in any client bundle (Google's explicit mandate).
21. **No npm deprecation warning for `@google/generative-ai`.** It's deprecated by docs/README only, not via `npm deprecate` — no install-time warning will appear. Migrate intentionally.

### E. Tailwind v4

22. **Verify `@supports` nested inside `@utility` compiles** (uncertain). Not documented. If it fails, declare the plain `clamp()` fallback and the `round()`-enhanced value as two declarations in the same `@utility` (later wins), or move `@supports` outside `@utility`. Test in a real build.
23. **Treat `scaleY(0.5)` hairline as 2x-optimized, not universally "1 device pixel."** On 1x it renders ~0.5 CSS px (anti-aliased); on 3x it isn't a single device pixel. If pixel-perfect single-device-px across 1x/1.5x/3x is required, gate the scale factor with `min-resolution` media queries.
24. **Confirm intent of px-locking `--spacing: 4px`** — it removes rem-based zoom scaling (accessibility tradeoff). The default `0.25rem` is already a 4px grid at a 16px root **and** scales with zoom.
25. **Decide closed vs extended palette.** `--color-*: initial` drops **all** default Tailwind colors (`bg-red-500` etc. disappear). Only do this for a truly closed palette.
26. **Confirm `Noto_Sans` / `Noto_Serif` export names + variable axes/subsets.** Docs examples use `Inter`/`Roboto_Mono`; Noto are standard Google Fonts but not shown by name. For a variable weight range you may need `weight: '100 900'` or rely on the variable default.

---

## 1. next-intl v4 + Next.js 16 (`proxy.ts`) i18n routing

### Canonical setup

Six files. Locales `['en','ko','es']`, `defaultLocale: 'en'`, `localePrefix: 'as-needed'` (English unprefixed; `/ko`, `/es` prefixed; dynamic `[slug]` stays English).

**`src/i18n/routing.ts`** ([routing config](https://next-intl.dev/docs/routing/configuration))
```ts
import {defineRouting} from 'next-intl/routing';

export const routing = defineRouting({
  locales: ['en', 'ko', 'es'],
  defaultLocale: 'en',
  localePrefix: 'as-needed'
});
```

**`src/i18n/request.ts`** — `locale` is **mandatory** in v4. ([getting started](https://next-intl.dev/docs/getting-started/app-router/with-i18n-routing), [v4 blog](https://next-intl.dev/blog/next-intl-4-0))
```ts
import {getRequestConfig} from 'next-intl/server';
import {hasLocale} from 'next-intl';
import {routing} from './routing';

export default getRequestConfig(async ({requestLocale}) => {
  const requested = await requestLocale;
  const locale = hasLocale(routing.locales, requested)
    ? requested
    : routing.defaultLocale;

  return {
    locale,
    messages: (await import(`../../messages/${locale}.json`)).default
  };
});
```

**`src/i18n/navigation.ts`** ([navigation](https://next-intl.dev/docs/routing/navigation))
```ts
import {createNavigation} from 'next-intl/navigation';
import {routing} from './routing';

export const {Link, redirect, usePathname, useRouter, getPathname} =
  createNavigation(routing);
```

**`src/proxy.ts`** — NOT `middleware.ts`. Import stays `next-intl/middleware`. ([proxy convention](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/proxy), [next-intl routing](https://next-intl.dev/docs/getting-started/app-router/with-i18n-routing))
```ts
import createMiddleware from 'next-intl/middleware';
import {routing} from './i18n/routing';

export default createMiddleware(routing);

export const config = {
  matcher: '/((?!api|trpc|_next|_vercel|.*\\..*).*)'
};
```

**`next.config.ts`** — path argument is optional (auto-detected). ([getting started](https://next-intl.dev/docs/getting-started/app-router))
```ts
import type {NextConfig} from 'next';
import createNextIntlPlugin from 'next-intl/plugin';

const nextConfig: NextConfig = {};

// './src/i18n/request.ts' is auto-detected; the argument is optional.
const withNextIntl = createNextIntlPlugin();
export default withNextIntl(nextConfig);
```

**`src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx`** — `params` is a Promise; `setRequestLocale` before any next-intl call; `NextIntlClientProvider` with **no** props (v4 auto-inherits messages/formats).
```tsx
import {NextIntlClientProvider, hasLocale} from 'next-intl';
import {notFound} from 'next/navigation';
import {setRequestLocale} from 'next-intl/server';
import {routing} from '@/i18n/routing';

export function generateStaticParams() {
  return routing.locales.map((locale) => ({locale}));
}

type Props = {children: React.ReactNode; params: Promise<{locale: string}>};

export default async function LocaleLayout({children, params}: Props) {
  const {locale} = await params;
  if (!hasLocale(routing.locales, locale)) notFound();
  setRequestLocale(locale);
  return (
    <html lang={locale}>
      <body>
        <NextIntlClientProvider>{children}</NextIntlClientProvider>
      </body>
    </html>
  );
}
```

**SEO — localized sitemap (note: `getPathname` is `await`ed in official example).** ([actions/metadata/route-handlers](https://next-intl.dev/docs/environments/actions-metadata-route-handlers))
```ts
import type {MetadataRoute} from 'next';
import {getPathname} from '@/i18n/navigation';
import {routing} from '@/i18n/routing';

const host = 'https://example.com';

async function entry(href: string) {
  return {
    url: host + (await getPathname({locale: routing.defaultLocale, href})),
    alternates: {
      languages: Object.fromEntries(
        await Promise.all(
          routing.locales.map(async (l) => [l, host + (await getPathname({locale: l, href}))])
        )
      )
    }
  };
}

export default async function sitemap(): Promise<MetadataRoute.Sitemap> {
  return Promise.all([entry('/'), entry('/about')]);
}
```

**Localized metadata** — pass explicit `locale` to keep static rendering.
```ts
import {getTranslations} from 'next-intl/server';

export async function generateMetadata({params}: {params: Promise<{locale: string}>}) {
  const {locale} = await params;
  const t = await getTranslations({locale, namespace: 'Metadata'});
  return {title: t('title')};
}
```

### DO / DON'T

- **DO** name the file `src/proxy.ts` and use `export default createMiddleware(routing)`. The import stays `next-intl/middleware`.
- **DO** `await params` in every layout, page, `generateMetadata`, and consumers of dynamic params.
- **DO** call `setRequestLocale(locale)` on **every** statically rendered layout/page, **after** the `hasLocale → notFound()` guard and **before** any other next-intl call.
- **DO** return `{locale, messages}` from `getRequestConfig` — `locale` is required.
- **DO** route all internal navigation through the `Link`/`redirect` from `navigation.ts` so the active locale persists; pass dynamic slugs via the href object: `<Link href={{pathname: '/blog/[slug]', params: {slug: 'hello'}}}>`.
- **DON'T** pass `messages` to `NextIntlClientProvider` (redundant in v4; `null` is the explicit opt-out).
- **DON'T** set the `runtime` segment-config option in `proxy.ts` — it throws (proxy defaults to Node.js runtime).
- **DON'T** add a `pathnames` map unless you want localized static segments. Dynamic `[slug]` values stay English in both cases.

### Gotchas that break on this stack

- **`middleware.ts` is deprecated/renamed to `proxy.ts` in Next 16.** Migration codemod: `npx @next/codemod@canary middleware-to-proxy .` ([proxy docs](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/proxy))
- **Matcher must catch `/` and all unprefixed paths** (required for `as-needed`). Without a matcher, proxy runs on every request including `_next/static` and `public/`. Use `/((?!api|trpc|_next|_vercel|.*\\..*).*)`.
- **`_next/data` routes still trigger proxy** even when excluded by a negative matcher — intentional (security). Usually harmless for next-intl.
- **`params` is a Promise** — forgetting `await` yields a Promise where a string is expected.
- **next-intl v4 is ESM-only** (except the `next-intl/plugin` entry). Min TypeScript 5, React 17+ JSX transform.
- **`usePathname`/`getPathname` return the un-substituted template** for dynamic routes (e.g. `/news/[articleSlug]`); the slug you pass through `Link` params is used verbatim and stays English.

⚠ **Residual uncertainty:** No official source fetched states an explicit minimum Next.js version for next-intl v4 — verify the installed peer-dependency range against Next 16. The `/en/about → /about` auto-redirect for `as-needed` was not quoted verbatim but is standard documented behavior. The `next-intl/plugin`-is-the-CJS-exception nuance was not separately verified (ESM-only is confirmed).

---

## 2. next-mdx-remote-client v2 — runtime MDX in App Router (RSC)

### Canonical setup

Install `next-mdx-remote-client@^2` (latest **2.1.11**) with React 19. RSC entry is `next-mdx-remote-client/rsc`. ([npm manifest](https://registry.npmjs.org/next-mdx-remote-client), [README](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ipikuka/next-mdx-remote-client/main/README.md))

**Two-path design.**

**Detail page (compile body + frontmatter)** — `evaluate` returns everything in one result:
```tsx
import {evaluate, type EvaluateOptions} from 'next-mdx-remote-client/rsc';
import type {MDXComponents} from 'next-mdx-remote-client/rsc';
import rehypeSlug from 'rehype-slug';
import rehypeAutolinkHeadings from 'rehype-autolink-headings';

// Generic order is <Frontmatter, Scope>; both MUST be `type` (not interface)
// and extend Record<string, unknown>.
type Frontmatter = {title: string; date: string};

const options: EvaluateOptions = {
  parseFrontmatter: true,           // vfile-matter, no gray-matter needed
  // disableImports/disableExports are TOP-LEVEL, not under mdxOptions:
  disableImports: true,
  disableExports: true,
  mdxOptions: {
    rehypePlugins: [rehypeSlug, [rehypeAutolinkHeadings, {behavior: 'wrap'}]]
  }
};

export default async function Page() {
  const source = docSnap.data().body_mdx as string; // or fs.readFile
  const {content, frontmatter, error} = await evaluate<Frontmatter>({
    source, options, components
  });
  if (error) return <ErrorComponent error={error} />;
  // Still wrap {content} in a React ErrorBoundary for render-time errors.
  return <article><h1>{frontmatter.title}</h1>{content}</article>;
}
```

**Components map** — explicit every time (RSC can't read `MDXProvider`):
```tsx
import {type MDXComponents} from 'next-mdx-remote-client/rsc';
import VideoPlayer from './VideoPlayer';
import MDXImage from './MDXImage';

export const components: MDXComponents = {
  VideoPlayer,
  MDXImage,
  img: (props) => <MDXImage {...props} />,
  wrapper: ({children}) => <div className="prose">{children}</div>
};
```

**Index/listing grid (no compile)** — `getFrontmatter` from `/utils`:
```ts
import {getFrontmatter} from 'next-mdx-remote-client/utils';

type Meta = {title: string; date: string; cover?: string};
const {frontmatter, strippedSource} = getFrontmatter<Meta>(source);
```

**Translation-validation pipeline (server)** — disable ESM, reject on parse error:
```ts
const {error} = await evaluate({
  source,
  options: {parseFrontmatter: true, disableImports: true, disableExports: true}
});
if (error) throw new Error(`Invalid MDX translation: ${error.message}`);
```

### DO / DON'T

- **DO** import from `next-mdx-remote-client/rsc` in App Router server components — it exposes the async `MDXRemote` component and async `evaluate` function.
- **DO** use `evaluate` when you need `frontmatter`/`scope`/`mod`; use `MDXRemote` with `onError={ErrorComponent}` when you only render content.
- **DO** set `parseFrontmatter: true` (uses `vfile-matter` internally — `gray-matter` is **not** a dependency).
- **DO** pass the `components` map on **every** call.
- **DO** wrap rendered `{content}` in a React `ErrorBoundary` in addition to `onError`/`error`.
- **DO** put `disableImports`/`disableExports` at the **top level** of options.
- **DON'T** import from `/csr` or the bare package root in server components — those are the Pages-Router path (`serialize`/`hydrate`/`MDXClient`), not `MDXRemote`/`evaluate`.
- **DON'T** rely on `<MDXProvider>` — effectless in App Router.
- **DON'T** try to set `outputFormat`, `Fragment`, `jsx`, `jsxs`, `jsxDEV`, `useMDXComponents`, or `providerImportSource` in `mdxOptions` — omitted/opinionated (`outputFormat` is forced to `'function-body'`).
- **DON'T** declare generics with `interface` — TS will reject (`does not satisfy Record<string, unknown>`).

### Gotchas that break on this stack

- **ESM-only** (`"type": "module"`). v2 requires react/react-dom `>= 19.1.0` and Node `>= 20.9.0`. React 18 means use v1.x (`legacy`, 1.1.8) instead.
- **`/rsc` vs `/csr` are isolated and spelled backwards.** Importing the wrong subpath is the most common mistake.
- **Internal eval only throws for SYNTAX errors.** Render-time errors are not in the `error` field — hence the mandatory `ErrorBoundary`.
- **Any custom JSX tag in the MDX must exist in `components`** or rendering throws. Add `recma-mdx-escape-missing-components` (a recma plugin) to default missing tags to `() => null`.
- **Security/RCE:** MDX compiles to executable JS via runtime eval. Firestore translation strings are editor-supplied — treat as untrusted: `disableImports`/`disableExports` (top-level) and consider `remark-mdx-remove-expressions`. A strict CSP without `'unsafe-eval'` in `script-src` breaks runtime MDX.
- **`source` is type `Compatible` (string | vfile).** You fetch/read the source yourself; build code around the type. Firestore strings and `fs.readFile` output are passed identically.

⚠ **Residual uncertainty:** The "library never touches the filesystem" line is paraphrase, not a verbatim doc quote (the functional conclusion is sound, derived from `source: Compatible`). **Turbopack behavior under Next 16 is undocumented** — validate against your real build. The "v1.x and v2.x have identical APIs and are both maintained" claim was not directly verifiable. The RCE/CSP `'unsafe-eval'` framing is reasonable engineering guidance, not a documented library guarantee.

---

## 3. Firebase App Hosting — `apphosting.yaml`, secrets, GitHub auto-deploy

### Canonical setup

Requires the **Blaze** (pay-as-you-go) plan. ([get started](https://firebase.google.com/docs/app-hosting/get-started)) Apps build with Cloud Build buildpacks (Next.js adapter), serve on Cloud Run, cache in Cloud CDN. ([about](https://firebase.google.com/docs/app-hosting/about-app-hosting))

**`apphosting.yaml`** — set `cpu` explicitly (default is `0`!). ([configure](https://firebase.google.com/docs/app-hosting/configure))
```yaml
runConfig:
  cpu: 1            # default is 0 — set explicitly for a guaranteed full CPU
  memoryMiB: 512    # range 128–32768; >4GiB needs >=2 CPUs, >8GiB >=4, etc.
  minInstances: 0
  maxInstances: 100
  concurrency: 80   # MUST be 1 if cpu < 1

env:
  # NEXT_PUBLIC_ vars: plain value, available at BUILD so Next inlines them.
  - variable: NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID
    value: my-project
    availability:
      - BUILD
      - RUNTIME

  # Server-side runtime secret (Cloud Secret Manager reference):
  - variable: GEMINI_API_KEY
    secret: gemini-api-key       # optional version pin: gemini-api-key@5
    availability:
      - RUNTIME
```

**Secrets via CLI** ([configure](https://firebase.google.com/docs/app-hosting/configure)):
```bash
firebase apphosting:secrets:set gemini-api-key
# For secrets created directly in Cloud Secret Manager, grant access explicitly:
firebase apphosting:secrets:grantaccess gemini-api-key
```

**firebase-admin uses ADC — no key file.** ([Firebase SDKs](https://firebase.google.com/docs/app-hosting/firebase-sdks))
```ts
import {initializeApp} from 'firebase-admin/app';
const app = initializeApp(); // ADC at build & run time; no service account key
```

**Continuous deployment:** set the live branch (e.g. `main`); automatic rollouts are on by default; each push to the live branch triggers a rollout. ([get started](https://firebase.google.com/docs/app-hosting/get-started))

### DO / DON'T

- **DO** upgrade to Blaze first.
- **DO** set `cpu` explicitly; if `cpu < 1`, set `concurrency: 1`.
- **DO** declare `NEXT_PUBLIC_` vars as plain `value` with `availability` including `BUILD` (so Next inlines them at build).
- **DO** reference runtime secrets via the per-env-var `secret` field (supports `@version` pinning).
- **DO** run `firebase apphosting:secrets:grantaccess` for secrets created outside the Firebase CLI.
- **DO** call `initializeApp()` with no arguments for firebase-admin (ADC).
- **DON'T** assume a 1-CPU default.
- **DON'T** put `NEXT_PUBLIC_` values in `secret` fields — they need to be plain build-time values to be inlined.
- **DON'T** rely on console env vars and `apphosting.yaml` being equal — **console values always override** YAML (hierarchy: console > env-specific YAML > base YAML).

### Gotchas that break on this stack

- **`cpu` default is 0** — the single biggest config trap (forces `concurrency: 1`).
- **Console env vars override `apphosting.yaml`** silently.
- **GitHub connection is a browser/console step.** `firebase init apphosting` only generates the YAML; it does not create the backend or connect GitHub.

⚠ **Residual uncertainty / refuted claims:**
- **"Next.js 16 unsupported" is REFUTED** — docs say "Next.js 13.5.x and higher" with no upper bound. Re-verify against the framework support schedule (the schedule page 404'd at the guessed URL during research).
- **"Developer Connect OAuth" naming is UNCERTAIN** — not found in the cited page; the GitHub-integration doc 404'd at the guessed URL. The connection is browser-based, but the exact mechanism/prerequisite APIs need confirmation.
- **"Skip the browser entirely" is UNCERTAIN/overstated** — no fully browser-free backend-creation path was verified; `firebase deploy` is not the documented primary deploy mechanism (rollouts are GitHub-push driven).
- **"Secrets can't back `NEXT_PUBLIC_`" is UNVERIFIED inference** — docs don't state it; the documented safe pattern (plain value + BUILD availability) is what matters.
- WebSearch was unavailable during research; verifications rest on direct WebFetch of official `firebase.google.com` pages.

---

## 4. Gemini API via `@google/genai` for MDX translation

### Canonical setup

Use **`@google/genai`** (current; latest **2.9.0**, published 2026-06-19, requires Node 20+). The old **`@google/generative-ai`** (latest 0.24.1, 2025-04-29) is deprecated. ([migration guide](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/migrate), [js-genai](https://github.com/googleapis/js-genai), [npm](https://registry.npmjs.org/@google/genai))

**Server-side only** — pass `apiKey` explicitly:
```ts
import {GoogleGenAI} from '@google/genai';

// Explicit apiKey: SDK auto-reads GEMINI_API_KEY OR GOOGLE_API_KEY,
// and GOOGLE_API_KEY WINS if both are set. Pass explicitly to avoid ambiguity.
const ai = new GoogleGenAI({apiKey: process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY});
```

### DO / DON'T

- **DO** install `@google/genai` and use the `GoogleGenAI` class (named import).
- **DO** pass `apiKey` explicitly to the constructor.
- **DO** run the SDK and key server-side only (build script or backend route) — Google's explicit guidance: "Never expose keys client-side in production... run a backend proxy server." ([API key docs](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/api-key))
- **DO** require Node `>= 20` in `package.json` engines / CI (and `apphosting.yaml`).
- **DON'T** rely on env auto-detection — `GOOGLE_API_KEY` silently wins over `GEMINI_API_KEY` if both are present.
- **DON'T** ship the key in any client/browser bundle.
- **DON'T** expect an `npm install` deprecation warning for `@google/generative-ai` — it's deprecated by docs only, not via `npm deprecate`.

### Gotchas that break on this stack

- **Two distinct backends, same SDK:** Gemini Developer API (AI Studio key, `apiKey`) vs Vertex AI on Google Cloud (ADC + project/location, **no plain apiKey**). For the simple env-key approach here, use an **AI Studio Gemini API key**.
- **Node 18 builds will fail** — `@google/genai` 2.9.0 mandates Node 20+.

⚠ **Residual uncertainty:** The task title's "Google Cloud API key" phrasing conflates the AI Studio (developer) and Vertex AI paths — decide explicitly before coding. Specify a concrete model name at call time (e.g. a current Gemini model id) — the research did not pin one, so confirm the exact model id against the current Gemini API model list.

---

## 5. Tailwind v4 — design tokens, integer-px fluid type, hairline border, Noto fonts

### Canonical setup

CSS-first: no `tailwind.config.js`. Single entry (e.g. `src/app/globals.css`). ([theme](https://tailwindcss.com/docs/theme), [v4 blog](https://tailwindcss.com/blog/tailwindcss-v4), [functions & directives](https://tailwindcss.com/docs/functions-and-directives), [adding custom styles](https://tailwindcss.com/docs/adding-custom-styles))

```css
@import "tailwindcss";

@theme {
  --spacing: 4px;                /* strict 4px grid: p-1=4 p-2=8 p-4=16 ... */
  --color-body: #1E1E1E;         /* bg-body / text-body / border-body */
  --color-hairline: #D9D9D9;     /* border-hairline */
}

/* map next/font vars — 'inline' so var-of-var resolves */
@theme inline {
  --font-sans: var(--font-noto-sans);
  --font-serif: var(--font-noto-serif);
}

/* px-locked type scale (pin size AND weight via @utility) */
@utility text-display { font-size: 40px; font-weight: 700; line-height: 1.1; }
@utility text-h1      { font-size: 28px; font-weight: 700; }
@utility text-h2      { font-size: 20px; font-weight: 700; }
@utility text-h3      { font-size: 16px; font-weight: 500; }
@utility text-body    { font-size: 14px; font-weight: 400; }

/* fluid, integer-px, 11px floor, with @supports fallback */
@utility fluid-body {
  font-size: clamp(11px, 0.6rem + 0.5vw, 14px);                 /* fallback */
  @supports (font-size: round(1px, 1px)) {
    font-size: round(clamp(11px, 0.6rem + 0.5vw, 14px), 1px);   /* enhanced */
  }
}

/* crisp ~1-device-px hairline (2x-optimized scaled pseudo-element) */
@utility hairline-b {
  position: relative;
  &::after {
    content: ""; position: absolute; inset: auto 0 0 0;
    border-bottom: 1px solid var(--color-hairline);
    transform: scaleY(0.5); transform-origin: 0 100%;
  }
}
```

**Fonts — `src/app/fonts.ts`** ([next/font](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/components/font)):
```ts
import {Noto_Sans, Noto_Serif} from 'next/font/google';

export const notoSans = Noto_Sans({
  subsets: ['latin'], display: 'swap', variable: '--font-noto-sans'
});
export const notoSerif = Noto_Serif({
  subsets: ['latin'], display: 'swap', variable: '--font-noto-serif'
});
```

**Root layout:**
```tsx
import {notoSans, notoSerif} from './fonts';

<html lang="en" className={`${notoSans.variable} ${notoSerif.variable}`}>
  <body className="font-sans bg-body antialiased">{children}</body>
</html>
```

### DO / DON'T

- **DO** declare `@theme` tokens at the **top level** (not nested under selectors, media queries, or `@layer`). Put responsive/dark overrides in `:root` or a regular selector.
- **DO** use `@theme inline` when a token references another CSS variable (e.g. a next/font variable) — this exact pattern is in both Tailwind and Next.js official docs.
- **DO** override `--spacing` to lock a px grid (`p-1=4px`, `p-2=8px`, …); utilities accept arbitrary multipliers out of the box (`p-13`, `w-17`).
- **DO** declare brand colors as exact-hex `--color-*` tokens — they generate the full utility family (`bg-`/`text-`/`border-`/`fill-`/`stroke-`/`ring-`).
- **DO** prefer `@utility` over redefining built-in `--text-*` tokens when you must pin **both** size and weight.
- **DO** call Noto Sans in the **root layout** so it preloads on all routes; Noto Serif preloads only where its `.variable`/className is applied.
- **DON'T** put `@theme` variables inside a selector/media-query/`@layer`.
- **DON'T** use `--color-*: initial` unless you want to delete **all** default Tailwind colors.
- **DON'T** assume `scaleY(0.5)` is a true 1-device-px line at every density (it's 2x-optimized).

### Gotchas that break on this stack

- **`round()` is Baseline 2024 "newly available"** (Safari 15.4+, Chrome/Edge 125+, Firefox 118+, Opera 111+; ~85.5% global) — **not** widely available. Ship the `@supports` fallback to plain `clamp()` through 2026. `clamp()` itself (Baseline since 2020) needs no fallback. ([round](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/round), [caniuse round](https://caniuse.com/mdn-css_types_round), [clamp](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/clamp))
- **`round(clamp(...), 1px)` snaps to whole CSS px, not device px** — exactly what you want for glyph metrics, but it doesn't guarantee device-pixel alignment on fractional-dppr (1.5x) screens. `round()` requires the `interval` argument for dimensions.
- **Overriding `--spacing` to `4px` removes rem/zoom scaling** — the default `0.25rem` is already a 4px grid at 16px root and scales with user zoom. Accessibility tradeoff.
- **Opacity modifiers on hex tokens** (e.g. `bg-body/50`) compute via `color-mix`/oklab — the base hex stays exact, but the mixed result isn't a naive hex alpha.

⚠ **Residual uncertainty (verify in a real build):**
- **`@supports` nested inside `@utility`** is not documented for Tailwind v4's compiler. Standard CSS nesting permits it, but if it fails, declare both font-size lines directly in the `@utility` (later wins) or move `@supports` outside.
- **The `scaleY(0.5)` hairline "recommended over media queries" framing is opinion**, not documented best practice. It's truly 1 device px only at exactly 2dppr.
- **`Noto_Sans`/`Noto_Serif` export names and variable axes/subsets** were not shown by name in docs (examples use `Inter`/`Roboto_Mono`) — confirm the family's variable support and whether you need `weight: '100 900'`.

---

**Key files to create (all absolute under the project `src/`):** `src/i18n/routing.ts`, `src/i18n/request.ts`, `src/i18n/navigation.ts`, `src/proxy.ts`, `next.config.ts`, `src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx`, `src/app/sitemap.ts`, `src/app/fonts.ts`, `src/app/globals.css`, `apphosting.yaml`.
